tasks.title: Jacob - Legendary - Weekly Leads & Insights Reporting For Client
LEGENDARY - UPDATES TO MAKE ON TRAFFIC
tasks.description: <div>couple updates for Legendary Services. Number one, they would like the sump pump campaign cut off. They're going to be dry and they feel like it's dried up, so to speak, with the campaigns. Secondly, look at any parts of the campaigns where we've spent $5.50 or higher and haven't generated any leads. And ideally, let's cut those off. And oh, by the way, I kind of think we ought to be doing that with every client campaign, not just this one. So when we meet tomorrow, ideally you can let me know what the guidelines are because this week especially we've seen several clients go hey why have we spent 700 1200 a thousand dollars and we're not seeing any leads so we need to be more proactive uh as i view it so this is for legendary specifically next they'd like to get the ac campaigns now in place so if you can get the team to spin up AC campaign prep and get those over right away. If it's just as easy to do these other two with it, great. But if it's going to delay it by longer than one extra business day, then just do the AC campaigns first. We'll get those approved while we do these next two behind the scenes. And the next two are related to plumbing as well. And one plumbing category are related to water softeners, right? So ideally look at proven campaigns in the marketplace and come up with some good angles related to water softeners. And then separately would be sewer cleaning and or pipe, you know, pipe draining, right? Where they're similar to Northern services. Ideally, you can lean on some of those. Okay, so that covers search campaigns. Also, search campaigns, they have expressed that we have narrowed the market compared to what their preferences are a little bit smaller than normal and I can understand the pros and cons of that but what they would like to do is make sure that we expand the zip codes to at least be in the zip codes of the two LSA accounts and also include the top northern part of Wisconsin. They service the southern part of Wisconsin on the northern end of Illinois about 15 miles, right? So we want to include those zip codes in the search as they were looking. And this is happening more and more because AI has made everybody an expert and everybody an analyst. And now, you know, like this is happening more and more and more. So we have to be far more proactive in my opinion. So that would be that situation. Also of note, the client gave us an agreed upon allocated budget for the month of 12-5. And we've already gone over that. I'm not sure why that happened or how that happened. It just can't happen. Now, the silver lining from it, by the way, for them anyway, is that they got a lot of great sales from it. However, they were like, why did we overspend? And, you know, we still have 10 days left in the month and we're already past our budget that we agreed to. So how would you answer that? And ideally, you can let me know what the thought thinking behind the thinking of that is so that I understand, you know, like what is being looked at? Why is this stuff not being managed and monitored? And again, I'm just relaying what clients are sharing. Again, I can come up with pros and cons to all these things and spin these things, but it shouldn't happen. We should not be this far over budget with 10 days left into the month. Now, these couple pruning things that we'll do, again, they don't want drastic changes. They're happy with the fact that those turn into sales. I think what I want to do is send you the Zoom call and you should probably listen to the Zoom call. This is reflective of almost all of the service clients and their mentality at this point and they're also curiosity and inquisiveness again AI has opened that Pandora's box in a lot of ways but these things got to be addressed so anyway those are the main action steps for you to be able to take to handle the campaigns. And I apologize that this is so long, but it needs to be thorough because when it's not, stuff's getting missed, it seems. And so I might be overcorrecting a little bit here, but at the end of the day, we need to solve some of these things and be on top of our game. Thanks.</div>

tasks.title: Review - Recommend NEW page for our Breakthrough Strategy Call
GHL Funnels and Workflows
tasks.description: <div><phmention contenteditable="false" class="mprofile" data-id="12896335931">Dan Kuschell </phmention> <phmention contenteditable="false" class="mprofile" data-id="12896349500">Phil </phmention><phmention contenteditable="false" class="mprofile" data-id="13027884616">Tracy Rye </phmention><phmention contenteditable="false" class="mprofile" data-id="12896376637">Abigail SW </phmention><br />Please add anyone involved in funnel creation on GHL.</div><div>It is important to use a consistent naming convention for each of our products both on the funnel as on the automation side. contains the funnels and pages for each product that way it is easier to reference.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div><strong>Regarding the funnel naming.</strong></div><div>I bring this up because we have the Direct Response Branding funnels and some are of the manes I see are:</div><div>Funnels</div><div>- Direct Response Branding Book</div><div>- Direct Response Book</div><div>- B3X - DRB - Evergreen</div><div>- DRB EVG Template</div><div>- DRB - Name Your Price (Folder)</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>I think we could use a naming convention like</div><div><strong>Creator Name or Initials - Product Name&nbsp; - Funel type</strong></div><div><br /><strong>Folders</strong></div><div>Using folders for each product is great, but currently, there are some funnels in folders, and some aren't so it's hard to track. The same goes for automation. All automations related to the same product should be in one folder.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>I don't understand the purpose of the numbering, some funnels have a number, and some don't. I use them sometimes to bring the active funnels to the top of the list but I don't see the purpose with the current numbering</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div><strong>Funnel creation</strong></div><div>When creating a sales funnel make sure each page that leads to a checkout has their own individual checkout, that way it is easier to see how they're performing from the funnel Stats tab.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>Currently the "B3x - Direct Response Branding - Book FREE + Shipping Campaign" has 1 main checkout page and multiple lead capture pages that send traffic to the checkout page. Because of this we can't see the sales generated from each individual effort in the stats page.&nbsp;</div><div>DO NOT send traffic from one funnel to a checkout page in a different funnel. Then it's even harder to figure out how the campaigns are performing. (it is currently happening with the book funnel.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div><strong>Name your price offers</strong></div><div>I would suggest creating a specific Thank you page for each "name your" price page, since the product sales aren't tracked on the funnel for this type of transaction. At least that way we can see while looking at the funnel how many people landed on the thank you page and "technically" those are the number of sales. Make sure these pages are no-index, no-follw</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div><strong>Documenting a project</strong><br />Please create a Google doc or spreadsheet that has each product/project:</div><div>- Funnels</div><div>- Automations</div><div>- Forms</div><div>- Tags</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>&nbsp;</div>
